    Setting Up And Trying AFK Agents Solution

    Let's run this and see what happens. You should be getting your agent immediately.

    You can control click to view the logs and now you can see the agent has started. The agent sees that phase one is already done and is now going to work on phase two.

    Watching the Agent Work

    The agent is now doing Agent(Explore repo) for phase two. You can see all of the tool calls here and everything is surfaced to you through the logs.

    Crucially, you can also see the iteration number that you're on here - iteration 1 out of 3. This tells you where the agent is in its execution cycle.

    I'm going to do something a little bit strange: step away for a minute and let this cook. I recommend you do the same. Go and get yourself a cup of tea, and come back to see what it actually did.

    What the Agent Built

    We've come back and we have working code.

    Now you have:

    • A revenue over time chart
    • User logged in as Alex Rivera
    • A per-course breakdown at the bottom

    You can filter by instructors, so you can filter by Marcus Johnson or filter by Sarah Chen. All of this looks good. The chart is moving in a slightly funny way, but you could pick that up in QA probably. This is looking pretty good.

    Diving Into the Logs

    Let's dive back through the logs to see what happened here.

    The logs show:

    • Phase one is already done
    • Phase two is the revenue over time chart (which was done in the interactive exercise)

    The agent kicks off an Agent(Explore repo), runs typecheck, runs tests, does some syntax error checking, and runs more typecheck. All the tests are passing and then it creates a commit.

    The agent is adding a recharts line chart to the admin analytics dashboard.

    Multiple Iterations

    If we zoom down in the logs, we can see the agent gets to iteration two out of three. The agent didn't see the completion signal in the previous iteration, so it:

    1. Sets up the sandbox again
    2. Confirms phase one and phase two are already done
    3. Explores the codebase
    4. Creates the course breakdown table with instructor filter

    All the tests pass and it creates the commit. Then at the bottom, we see no more tasks being emitted.

    The agent grabbed all of the commits and signaled completion after two iterations.

    The Result

    You were fully able to go and get yourself a cup of tea and come back to working code. You can see lots more tests were written.

    All of your instincts about planning are paying off here. You just align the agent and then point it in a direction, and then you're good to go. You get working code at the end of it.

    The Future of Development

    This is the future of development. You're able to parallelize yourself with the agent. Having these two streams running at the same time, you doing something else, planning future work, and the agent working on something, has been pretty cool.
